title: Delicate Molten Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A romantic Tunisian dish featuring bacon lardon and cooked in a cast iron skillet, this recipe is onion and garlic free, making it perfect for those with sensitivities, and is characterized by its floral and molten characteristics.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a cast iron skillet over medium high heat.
Add the diced bacon lardon to the skillet and cook until crispy.
Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside.
Add the lamb pieces to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the chopped apricots, prunes, and chicken broth to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the bacon to the skillet and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the skillet with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook the tagine for 2 1/2 hours or until the lamb is tender and the sauce is molten.
